name: canvaslms
documentation:
# canvaslms: A CLI to Canvas LMS.

This program provides a command-line interface for Canvas. The command
is `canvaslms` and it has several subcommands in the same style as Git.
`canvaslms` provides output in a format useful for POSIX tools, this
makes automating tasks much easier.

## Getting started

Start by login to your Canvas server

``` {.text}
canvaslms login
```

Let's consider how to grade students logging into the student-shell SSH
server. We store the list of students' Canvas and KTH IDs in a file.

``` {.text}
canvaslms users -c DD1301 -s | cut -f 1,2 > students.csv
```

Then we check who has logged into student-shell.

``` {.text startFrom="2"}
ssh student-shell.sys.kth.se last | cut -f 1 -d " " | sort | uniq \
  > logged-in.csv
```

Finally, we check who of our students logged in.

``` {.text startFrom="4"}
for s in $(cut -f 2 students.csv); do
  grep $s logged-in.csv && \
```

Finally, we can set their grade to P and add the comment "Well done!" in
Canvas. We set the grades for the two assignments whose titles match the
regular expression `(Preparing the terminal|The terminal)`.

``` {.text startFrom="6"}
    canvaslms grade -c DD1301 -a "(Preparing the terminal|The terminal)" \
      -u $(grep $s students.csv | cut -f 1) \
      -g P -m "Well done!"
done
```

## Installation

Just install the PyPI package:
```
python3 -m pip install canvaslms
```
Some subcommands use `pandoc`, so you will likely have to [install 
pandoc][pandoc] on your system manually.

[pandoc]: https://pandoc.org/installing.html

modules.name: canvaslms.grades.maxgradesurvey
documentation:
Module that summarizes an assignment group by maximizing grade and date. This 
module is the same as `canvaslms.grades.disjunctmax`, but also includes 
ungraded surveys (for instance quiz with points, where the number of points is 
ignored). Consequently all assignments must have a date.

This function also doen't fail when there is a grade other than A--F or P/F 
present. Such grades are all treated as F.

functions.name: canvaslms.grades.maxgradesurvey.summarize
documentation:
Extracts user's submissions for assignments in assingments_list to
summarize results into one grade and a grade date. Summarize by disjunctive
maximum.

functions.name: canvaslms.grades.maxgradesurvey.summarize_group
documentation:
Summarizes a particular set of assignments (assignments_list) for all
users in users_list

modules.name: canvaslms.grades.tilkryLAB1
documentation:
Summarizes the assignments of LAB1 in the course DD2520 Applied Crypto at KTH.

There are some mandatory assignments graded P/F. They must all be P.

There are also some optional assigments. They're given scores between 0 and 
2.5. The sum of the scores is used to determine the grade. If the sum is more 
than 4, the grade is A.

functions.name: canvaslms.grades.tilkryLAB1.summarize
documentation:
Extracts user's submissions for assignments in assingments_list to summarize
results into one grade and a grade date.

Summarize according to tilkry grading scheme.

functions.name: canvaslms.grades.tilkryLAB1.summarize_group
documentation:
Summarizes a particular set of assignments (assignments_list) for all
users in users_list

modules.name: canvaslms.grades.disjunctmax
documentation:
Module that summarizes an assignment group by disjunctive maximum.

Disjunctive maximum means: 

  1) At least one assignment must have a non-F grade.
  2) If there are more than one assignment with a non-F grade, we take the 
     maximum as the grade. A--E are valued higher than P. The grade F is valued 
     the lowest.

We fail if there is an assignment which doesn't have A--F or P/F grading 
scales.

functions.name: canvaslms.grades.disjunctmax.summarize
documentation:
Extracts user's submissions for assignments in assingments_list to
summarize results into one grade and a grade date. Summarize by disjunctive
maximum.

functions.name: canvaslms.grades.disjunctmax.grade_max
documentation: Takes a list of A--E/P--F grades, returns the maximum.

functions.name: canvaslms.grades.disjunctmax.summarize_group
documentation:
Summarizes a particular set of assignments (assignments_list) for all
users in users_list

modules.name: canvaslms.grades.conjunctavgsurvey
documentation:
This module is the same as `canvaslms.grades.conjunctavg` except that any 
submissions with grades other than A--F and P/F are treated as P. For instance, 
numeric grades (like points). Also, all submissions must have a date. This 
makes this module useful for including mandatory, ungraded surveys.

functions.name: canvaslms.grades.conjunctavgsurvey.summarize
documentation:
Extracts user's submissions for assignments in assingments_list to summarize
results into one grade and a grade date. Summarize by conjunctive average.

If some submission lacks date, return ("F", None).

functions.name: canvaslms.grades.conjunctavgsurvey.summarize_group
documentation:
Summarizes a particular set of assignments (assignments_list) for all
users in users_list

modules.name: canvaslms.grades.conjunctavg
documentation:
Module that summarizes an assignment group by conjunctive average.

Conjunctive average means: 

  1) We need all assignments to have a non-F grade.
  2) If there are A--F assignments present, we will compute the average of 
     those grades. For instance; an A and a C will result in a B; an A and a B 
     will result in an A, but an A with two Bs will become a B (standard 
     rounding).

functions.name: canvaslms.grades.conjunctavg.summarize
documentation:
Extracts user's submissions for assignments in assingments_list to
summarize results into one grade and a grade date. Summarize by conjunctive
average.

functions.name: canvaslms.grades.conjunctavg.a2e_average
documentation: Takes a list of A--E grades, returns the average.

functions.name: canvaslms.grades.conjunctavg.summarize_group
documentation:
Summarizes a particular set of assignments (assignments_list) for all
users in users_list

modules.name: canvaslms.grades
documentation:
This package contains modules to summarize assignment groups in different ways. 
These modules are used with the `-S` option of the `results` command.

For a module to be used with the `canvaslms results -S module` option, the 
module must fulfil the following:

  1) It must contain a function named `summarize_group`.
  2) `summarize_group` must take two arguments:

       I) `assignment_list`, a list of `canvasapi.assignment.Assignment`
          objects. These assignments all belong to the same group, \ie their
          grades should be used to compute the student's final grade.

      II) `users_list`, a list of `canvasapi.user.User` objects. This is a
          list of users, i.e. students, for whom to compute the grades.

  3) The return value should be a list of lists. Each list should have the
     form `[user, grade, grade date, grader 1, ..., grader N]`.

For more details, see Chapter 11 of the `canvaslms.pdf` file found among the 
release files at:

  https://github.com/dbosk/canvaslms/releases

modules.name: canvaslms.hacks.canvasapi
documentation: A module that modifies the classes of the canvasapi package

functions.name: canvaslms.hacks.canvasapi.make_classes_comparable
documentation: Improves the classes by adding __eq__ and __hash__ methods

functions.name: canvaslms.hacks.canvasapi.make_useful_user_dunder_str
documentation: Improves the user class by changing __str__

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.grade.add_command
documentation: Adds grade command to the argparse subparser subp

modules.name: canvaslms.cli
documentation: A command-line interface for the Canvas LMS.

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.err
documentation:
Prints msg to stderr, prints a stack trace and
exits with rc as return code

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.warn
documentation: Prints msg to stderr

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.read_configuration
documentation: Returns a dictionary containing the configuration

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.update_config_file
documentation: Updates the config file by writing the config dictionary back to it

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.results.filter_grade
documentation: Returns True if the grade matches the regex.

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.results.summarize_assignments
documentation:
Turn submissions into results:
- canvas is a Canvas object,
- args is the command-line arguments, as parsed by argparse.

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.results.round_to_day
documentation: Takes a Canvas timestamp and returns the corresponding datetime.date object.

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.results.all_graders
documentation:
Returns a list of everyone who participated in the grading of the submission.
I.e. also those who graded previous submissions, when submission history is
available.

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.results.summarize_assignment_groups
documentation:
Summarize assignment groups into a single grade:
- canvas is a Canvas object,
- args is the command-line arguments, as parsed by argparse.

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.results.load_module
documentation: Load a module from the file system or a built-in module.

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.results.missing_assignments
documentation:
Returns tuples of missing assignments.

For each assignment that a student is not done with, we yield a tuple of the
user, the assignment and the reason why the assignment is missing.

The reason can be "not submitted" or "not graded" or "not a passing grade".

The only reason to use a different module is if you have optional assignments.
We only want to remind the students of the things they need to pass the course.
We don't want to make it sound like an optional assignment is mandatory.

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.results.add_command
documentation: Adds the results command to argparse parser subp

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.courses.courses_command
documentation: Prints the users list of courses in CSV format to stdout

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.courses.process_course_option

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.assignments.filter_assignments_by_group

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.assignments.format_assignment
documentation: Returns an assignment formatted for the terminal

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.assignments.format_rubric

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.assignments.format_json

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.assignments.list_assignments

Lists all assignments in all assignments containers (courses or
assignement groups)

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.assignments.filter_assignments

Returns all assignments from assignments_container whose
title matches regex

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.assignments.add_assignment_command
documentation: Adds the assignment subcommand to argparse parser subp

documentation: Guides the user to update credentials

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.login.load_credentials
documentation: Load credentials from keyring, environment or config dictionary

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.login.add_command
documentation: Adds the login command to argparse parser

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.users.add_users_command
documentation: Adds the users subcommand and its options to argparse subparser subp

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.users.make_user_rows
documentation: Takes a list of courses and returns a list of users in those courses

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.users.make_user_rows_w_groups
documentation:
Takes a list of courses and returns a list of users in those courses,
includes a group column

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.users.add_groups_command
documentation: Adds the groups subcommand and its options to argparse subparser subp

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.users.filter_group_categories
documentation:
Filters out the group categories whose names match regex
in the courses in course_list

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.users.filter_groups
documentation:
Items is a list of either courses or group categories,
regex is a regular expression.
Returns all groups whose name match regex.

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.users.list_users
documentation: List users in courses with roles

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.users.filter_users
documentation:
Filter users in courses with roles based on regex. `regex` is matched on
- Canvas ID (exact match),
- name (regex),
- login ID (regex),
- integration id (exact match),
- SIS user ID (exact match).

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.users.list_students
documentation: List users in courses with role student

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.users.list_teachers
documentation: List users in courses with role teacher

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.users.get_uid
documentation:
Takes a user object and returns a unique identifier.

Returns one of login_id, integration_id, sis_user_id, id (Canvas ID); in that
order of preference.

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.users.add_user_option_wo_depends
documentation:
Adds the -u option to argparse parser, without adding
other required options.

The `role` option allows specifying which roles to include, for instance
students or TAs.

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.users.add_user_option
documentation: Adds the -u option to argparse parser

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.users.process_user_option
documentation: Processes the user option from command line, returns a list of users

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.users.add_group_category_option_wo_depends
documentation: Adds the group category option, without adding required other options

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.users.add_group_category_option
documentation: Adds the group category option, adds required options

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.users.add_group_option_wo_depends
documentation:
Adds group filtering option to argparse parser,
without adding required options

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.users.add_group_option
documentation:
Adds group filtering options to argparse parser,
adds required other options

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.users.process_group_options
documentation: Processes the group/group category options, returns a list of groups

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.users.add_user_or_group_option
documentation: Adds user and group options as mutually exclusive options to parser

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.users.process_user_or_group_option
documentation: Returns a list of users, filtered either by user regex or by groups

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.users.add_command
documentation: Adds the subcommands users and groups to argparse subparser subp

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.submissions.speedgrader
documentation: Returns the SpeedGrader URL of the submission

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.submissions.format_submission
documentation:
Formats submission for printing to stdout. Returns a string.

If history is True, include all submission versions from history.

If json_format is True, return a JSON string, otherwise Markdown.

`md_title_level` is the level of the title in Markdown, by default `#`. This
is used to create a hierarchy of sections in the output.

`tmpdir` is the directory to store all the submission files. Defaults to None,
which creates a temporary directory.

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.submissions.format_section
documentation:
In the case of Markdown (default), we format the title as a header and the body
as a paragraph. If we don't do JSON, but receive a dictionary as the body, we
format it as a list of key-value pairs.

`md_title_level` is the level of the title in Markdown, by default `#`.
We'll use this to create a hierarchy of sections in the output.

In the case of JSON, we return a dictionary with the title as the key and the
body as the value.

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.submissions.resolve_grader
documentation:
Returns a user object if the submission was graded by a human.
Otherwise returns None if ungraded or a descriptive string.

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.submissions.convert_to_md
documentation:
Converts `attachment` to Markdown. Returns the Markdown string.

Store a file version in `tmpdir`.

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.submissions.text_to_md
documentation:
Takes a text-based content type, returns Markdown code block type.
Raises ValueError if not possible.

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.submissions.format_rubric
documentation:
Format the rubric assessment of the `submission` in readable form.

If `json_format` is True, return a JSON string, otherwise Markdown.

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.submissions.get_criterion
documentation: Returns criterion with ID `criterion_id` from rubric `rubric`

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.submissions.get_rating
documentation: Returns rating with ID `rating_id` from rubric criterion `criterion`

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.submissions.add_command
documentation: Adds the submissions and submission commands to argparse parser subp

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.submissions.add_submissions_command
documentation: Adds submissions command to argparse parser subp

functions.name: canvaslms.cli.submissions.add_submission_command
documentation: Adds submission command to argparse parser subp
